title: 7 Best Herbal Teas For Jaw Pain
intro: Herbal teas for jaw pain are a natural remedy used to alleviate discomfort and inflammation associated with temporomandibular joint disorders (TMJ). These teas can treat the ailment due to their anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and soothing properties, which help to reduce pain and swelling in the jaw area. Examples of herbal teas used to treat jaw pain include ginger tea, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce pain and inflammation; peppermint tea, which has a cooling effect that can help to numb the pain; chamomile tea, which has a soothing effect that can help to calm the mind and body; lavender tea, which has a calming effect that can help to reduce stress and anxiety that may be contributing to jaw pain; and turmeric tea, which contains curcumin that has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Additionally, other herbal teas such as eucalyptus, lemon balm, and rosemary tea may also be used to treat jaw pain due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.

preparations.herb_desc: Zingiber officinale teas, also known as ginger tea, have been traditionally used to treat jaw pain ailments, such as temporomandibular joint disorder (TMJ). The anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties of this herbal preparation help to reduce pain and inflammation in the jaw, thereby alleviating discomfort and promoting relaxation. The bioactive constituents of ginger tea, including gingerols and shogaols, possess potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help to reduce pain and inflammation in the jaw. Regular consumption of ginger tea has been shown to provide relief from jaw pain and improve overall oral health, making it a popular natural remedy for TMJ sufferers.
preparation_procedure: Get 1 teaspoon of dried Zingiber officinale (ginger) root powder.
Boil 1 cup of water in a pot.
Add 1 teaspoon of ginger powder to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid and drink when cool enough.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Arthritis and rheumatism", Zingiber officinale teas may not be specifically studied for jaw pain, but based on the study, a highly concentrated extract of Zingiber officinale, along with Alpinia galanga, effectively reduced symptoms of osteoarthritis, which includes pain, suggesting potential benefits for jaw pain due to its anti-inflammatory properties, although direct evidence for jaw pain specifically is lacking.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Zingiber officinale teas can be used to treat jaw pain ailments such as temporomandibular joint disorder, but possible side effects include stomach upset, dizziness, and nausea, especially when consumed in large quantities or without proper preparation. To minimize the risk of side effects, individuals should start with small doses, avoid consuming the tea on an empty stomach, and monitor their body's response to the herbal preparation to adjust their usage accordingly.
preparation_usage: Steep 1 teaspoon of dried ginger in 1 cup of hot water for 5-7 minutes.
Drink 1 cup of ginger tea 2-3 times a day for jaw pain relief.
Use 1/2 teaspoon of dried ginger powder as an alternative to dried ginger.
Add honey or lemon to taste if desired, but not necessary.
Continue for 2-5 days to experience full relief from jaw pain.

preparations.herb_desc: Echinacea purpurea teas have been traditionally used to treat jaw pain, particularly temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders, due to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The bioactive constituents of Echinacea purpurea, including alkylamides and caffeic acid derivatives, help to reduce inflammation and pain in the jaw area by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes. Drinking Echinacea purpurea teas can provide relief from jaw pain by reducing muscle tension, promoting relaxation, and alleviating symptoms associated with TMJ disorders. The benefits of Echinacea purpurea teas in treating jaw pain include reduced inflammation, improved mood, and enhanced overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Echinacea purpurea roots and flowers.
Boil 1 cup of water in a pot.
Add the Echinacea roots and flowers to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink 1/2 cup, 2-3 times a day.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Echinacea purpurea teas can be used to treat jaw pain, but possible side effects may include dizziness, nausea, and stomach upset, especially when consumed in large quantities or for an extended period. To minimize potential side effects, it is recommended to use Echinacea purpurea teas in moderation and avoid consuming them if you are allergic to plants in the daisy family or have any underlying medical conditions such as kidney disease or liver problems.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Echinacea purpurea tea 2-3 times a day.
Take 1 teaspoon of dried Echinacea purpurea per 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep for 5-7 minutes before straining and drinking.
Use 1-2 tablespoons of Echinacea purpurea tea per dose for jaw pain relief.
Continue for 7-10 days, or as directed by a healthcare professional.

preparations.herb_desc: Lavandula angustifolia teas, also known as English lavender tea, have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including jaw pain. The anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties of this herbal preparation help to reduce pain and inflammation in the jaw area, making it a potential remedy for conditions such as temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorder. The bioactive constituents, including linalool and linalyl acetate, are responsible for the tea's therapeutic effects, which help to relax muscles, reduce tension, and improve sleep quality, all of which can contribute to alleviating jaw pain. The benefits of consuming Lavandula angustifolia teas to treat jaw pain include reduced pain and inflammation, improved sleep quality, and relaxation of the jaw muscles, making it a natural and non-invasive treatment option.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of boiling water and 1 tablespoon of dried Lavandula angustifolia flowers.
Measure 1 tablespoon of honey (optional) and set aside.
Pour boiling water over the dried flowers in a cup or teapot.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the flowers using a tea strainer or a piece of cheesecloth.
Add honey to taste, if desired, and drink slowly to soothe jaw pain.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Heliyon", Lavandula angustifolia teas may be beneficial for alleviating jaw pain as lavender aromatherapy, when used in conjunction with an inferior alveolar nerve block, showed significant differences in pain levels compared to the block alone.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Lavandula angustifolia teas can be used to treat jaw pain ailments, but possible side effects may include drowsiness, stomach upset, and allergic reactions such as skin irritation or respiratory issues. To minimize these risks, individuals should start with small doses, monitor their body's response, and avoid consuming Lavandula angustifolia teas before driving or operating heavy machinery, especially in the morning or evening when the sedative properties may be more pronounced.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Lavandula angustifolia tea 2-3 times a day.
Start with a small dose of 1 teaspoon per cup and adjust as needed.
Gargle with tea for 30 seconds to reduce jaw pain and inflammation.
Swallow the tea and drink another cup as needed throughout the day.
Use Lavandula angustifolia tea for up to 2 weeks for optimal jaw pain relief.

preparations.herb_desc: Valeriana officinalis teas are a popular herbal remedy used to treat jaw pain, also known as temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorder, due to their analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and sedative properties. The bioactive constituents of this herbal preparation, including valerenic acid, isovaleric acid, and valeranone, help to reduce muscle spasms, alleviate pain, and promote relaxation, ultimately providing relief from jaw pain. The sedative properties of Valeriana officinalis teas also help to reduce stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for TMJ disorder. By promoting relaxation and reducing pain and inflammation, Valeriana officinalis teas offer a natural and non-invasive approach to treating jaw pain, making them a popular choice for those seeking a holistic remedy.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of boiling water and 2-3 teaspoons of dried Valeriana officinalis root.
Measure 1 tablespoon of honey (optional) and set aside.
Steep the Valeriana officinalis root in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the solids.
Add the measured honey (if using) and stir well to dissolve. Drink the tea slowly for relief from jaw pain.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Valeriana officinalis teas can be used to treat jaw pain, but it may cause side effects such as drowsiness, headaches, and stomach upset in some individuals, particularly when consumed in large quantities. To minimize potential side effects, it's essential to start with small amounts and gradually increase the dose, avoid consuming it before bedtime, and be cautious when combining it with other medications or substances that can interact with it.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Valeriana officinalis tea for jaw pain
Steep 1 teaspoon of dried Valeriana officinalis in a cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes
Drink 1-2 cups of Valeriana officinalis tea 3-4 times a day as needed
Start with a low dose of 1/2 teaspoon and gradually increase to 1 teaspoon if needed
Use Valeriana officinalis tea for 7-10 days to manage jaw pain and inflammation

preparations.herb_desc: Melissa officinalis teas have been traditionally used to treat jaw pain ailments, particularly temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders, due to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat jaw pain by reducing inflammation and muscle spasms in the jaw area, promoting relaxation and reducing discomfort. The bioactive constituents of Melissa officinalis teas, including rosmarinic acid and ursolic acid, have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which contribute to their therapeutic effects on jaw pain. The benefits of using Melissa officinalis teas to treat jaw pain include reduced inflammation, improved sleep quality, and enhanced overall well-being, making it a popular natural remedy for TMJ disorders.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Melissa officinalis leaves or 1 teaspoon of dried leaves.
Boil 1 cup of water in a pot.
Remove the pot from heat and add the Melissa officinalis leaves.
Steep the leaves for 5-7 minutes, then strain the tea into a cup.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help with jaw pain.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Journal of ethnopharmacology", Melissa officinalis teas may be beneficial for jaw pain due to the presence of biologically active compounds that modulate KATP, opioidergic, and serotonergic pathways, which have anti-nociceptive properties.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Melissa officinalis teas can be used to treat jaw pain, but possible side effects may include dizziness, stomach upset, and allergic reactions. Precautions to take when using Melissa officinalis teas to treat jaw pain include starting with a low dose, avoiding consumption before bedtime due to potential sedative effects, and monitoring for any signs of allergic reactions such as rash or difficulty breathing.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Melissa officinalis tea 3 times a day.
Take 5-10 drops of Melissa officinalis tincture 3 times a day.
Add 1 teaspoon of Melissa officinalis powder to 1 cup of water.
Drink Melissa officinalis tea or take the tincture before meals.
Use Melissa officinalis products for 2-4 weeks to see jaw pain relief.

preparations.herb_desc: Achillea millefolium teas, also known as yarrow tea, have been traditionally used to treat jaw pain and other temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders. The anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties of yarrow tea, primarily due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, help to reduce pain and inflammation in the jaw area. The bioactive constituents of yarrow tea, including achilleine and santonin, contribute to its therapeutic effects by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and promoting relaxation in the jaw muscles. By consuming yarrow tea, individuals can experience relief from jaw pain and other TMJ symptoms, making it a potential natural remedy for this common health issue.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Achillea millefolium leaves and flowers.
Measure 1 tablespoon of the gathered leaves and flowers and put them in a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the leaves and flowers in the tea infuser or cup.
Let the tea steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain it into another cup using a tea strainer or cheesecloth.
Drink 1/2 to 1 cup of the Achillea millefolium tea 2-3 times a day for jaw pain relief.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Achillea millefolium teas can be used to treat jaw pain, but possible side effects may include gastrointestinal issues such as nausea and diarrhea, as well as allergic reactions like hives or itching. Precautions to take when using Achillea millefolium teas to treat jaw pain include being cautious with the dosage and avoiding consumption if pregnant or breastfeeding, as well as potential interactions with other medications.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Achillea millefolium tea for jaw pain
Drink 1 cup of Achillea millefolium tea 2-3 times a day
Use 1-2 teaspoons of dried herbs per cup of boiling water
Drink tea for 5-7 days to experience potential relief
Monitor and adjust dosage as advised by your doctor or healthcare provider

preparations.herb_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas have been traditionally used to treat temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorder and associated jaw pain due to their anti-inflammatory and anti-arthritic properties. The herbal preparation helps to reduce pain and inflammation by modulating the body's response to stress and promoting relaxation. The bioactive constituents of Glycyrrhiza glabra, including flavonoids, glycosides, and triterpenes, contribute to its analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which alleviate jaw pain and discomfort. Regular consumption of Glycyrrhiza glabra teas can provide relief from TMJ-related jaw pain and improve overall quality of life, making it a beneficial herbal remedy for managing this ailment.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of water and 1 teaspoon of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root. You can buy this root at a health store or online.
Heat the water in a pot over low heat. Bring it to a simmer, but not a boil.
Add the dried root to the water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes. You can let it steep for a longer time if you prefer a stronger tea.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or a tea strainer. Discard the root.
Drink the tea warm or at room temperature. You can add honey to taste, but be careful not to add too much as Glycyrrhiza glabra can be sweet.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the study on herbal remedies for oral mucositis, Glycyrrhiza glabra teas may provide relief for jaw pain due to its anti-nociceptive properties, which help alleviate pain.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas can be used to treat jaw pain, but potential side effects include high blood pressure, low potassium levels, and water retention due to its licorice root content. When using these teas to treat jaw pain, it is essential to be cautious of excessive consumption, as it may lead to an imbalance of electrolytes in the body, and to drink plenty of water to help mitigate any adverse effects.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Glycyrrhiza glabra tea 2-3 times a day
Start with 1/2 teaspoon of dried root per cup of boiling water
Adjust dosage to 1 teaspoon per cup if needed after 2 days
Use tea for 7-10 days for optimal jaw pain relief
Consult a doctor if jaw pain persists after 10 days of use

preparations.herb_desc: Ginkgo biloba teas have been traditionally used to treat jaw pain ailments, such as temporomandibular joint disorders (TMJ), due to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The bioactive constituents, including flavonoids and terpenoids, in Ginkgo biloba teas help to reduce inflammation and pain in the jaw area by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and interacting with opioid receptors. Regular consumption of Ginkgo biloba teas has been found to provide relief from jaw pain and improve overall oral health by reducing swelling and promoting relaxation of jaw muscles. The benefits of using Ginkgo biloba teas to treat jaw pain include reduced pain and inflammation, improved sleep quality, and enhanced overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of boiling water and 1 teaspoon of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves.
Measure 1 tablespoon of honey (optional) for sweetness.
Add 1 teaspoon of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves to a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Pour the boiling water over the Ginkgo biloba leaves and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and add honey (if using) before drinking to help alleviate jaw pain.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Refu'at ha-peh veha-shinayim (1993), Ginkgo biloba teas for jaw pain are not mentioned as a treatment option in the given study, which focuses on the potential risks of commonly used herbal medications.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Ginkgo biloba teas can be used to treat jaw pain, but it may cause potential side effects such as dizziness, nausea, and stomach upset in some individuals due to its ability to thin blood and interact with other medications. To minimize potential risks, it is advised to start with a low dose and monitor your body's response, as well as avoid taking Ginkgo biloba teas with other medications or substances that may interact with it, such as blood thinners or certain antidepressants.

preparations.herb_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis teas, a herbal remedy, have been used to treat jaw pain ailments such as temporomandibular joint disorder (TMJ). The anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties of this herbal preparation help to reduce pain and inflammation in the jaw joint. The bioactive constituents of Rosmarinus officinalis teas, including rosmarinic acid and carnosic acid, contribute to its analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which help to alleviate jaw pain and discomfort. Regular consumption of Rosmarinus officinalis teas has been shown to provide relief from jaw pain and promote relaxation, making it a beneficial herbal remedy for individuals suffering from TMJ and related jaw pain ailments.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Rosmarinus officinalis leaves (or 1 teaspoon of dried leaves) and 1 cup of boiling water.
Measure 1 tablespoon of honey (optional) for sweetness.
Combine the Rosmarinus officinalis leaves and boiling water in a heat-resistant cup.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the leaves from the tea.
Drink the tea hot or warm, with or without honey, to help relieve jaw pain.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the study, I can't answer the question about Rosmarinus officinalis teas for jaw pain because the study does not mention Rosmarinus officinalis teas, but it does mention rosmarinic acid, which is a compound found in the extract of Opuntia ficus indica and possibly in Rosmarinus officinalis.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Rosmarinus officinalis teas can be used to potentially alleviate jaw pain, but it may cause side effects such as increased heart rate, nausea, and dizziness in some individuals. preparations.herb_desc: Camellia sinensis teas, including green and black tea, have been traditionally used to treat jaw pain and inflammation, particularly in cases of temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of Camellia sinensis teas, largely attributed to its bioactive constituents such as theaflavins and thearubigins, help to reduce pain and inflammation in the jaw area. By consuming Camellia sinensis teas, individuals may experience relief from jaw pain due to its ability to relax the muscles and reduce inflammation, thereby improving overall mouth and jaw function. The benefits of using Camellia sinensis teas to treat jaw pain include reduced pain and inflammation, improved jaw mobility, and a decrease in the risk of developing TMJ disorders.
preparation_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup Camellia sinensis leaves (black, green, or white), 1 cup water, and a strainer.
Heat water to 160-170°F (71-77°C). Not boiling.
Add 1 tablespoon Camellia sinensis leaves to the strainer. Pour hot water over the leaves.
Steep for 3-5 minutes. Then, remove the strainer with the leaves.
Drink 1 cup of the tea, 2-3 times a day, for relief from jaw pain.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Journal of ethnopharmacology, Camellia sinensis teas for jaw pain may have some benefit due to the presence of polyphenols, essential oils, and alkaloids with identified antibiofilm activities, although this study does not specifically focus on jaw pain but rather on its application as an antiplaque agent.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Camellia sinensis teas can be used to treat jaw pain, but possible side effects may include dizziness, nausea, and stomach upset due to its caffeine content, as well as allergic reactions such as hives or itchy skin in rare cases. When using Camellia sinensis teas to treat jaw pain, precautions should be taken to avoid consuming excessive amounts, especially for those with sensitive stomachs or caffeine intolerance, and to discontinue use if any side effects occur.

Take 1 cup of the strained mixture and add 1 tablespoon of honey to taste. Stir well.
Drink the Salvia officinalis tea warm to help with jaw pain. You can drink it 2-3 times a day.
Consult a doctor or a healthcare professional before using Salvia officinalis for medicinal purposes, especially if you have any underlying health conditions.

This herbal preparation helps to treat jaw pain by relaxing tense muscles, reducing inflammation, and relieving pain through its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
